{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - revision.comment: fixing spacing error
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 9.68 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 44.65 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial_structure = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South |label = Nishinorikura Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (前方後円墳) and part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}}. The site is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped burial mound constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}}, it forms a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} and {{ill|Shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, forming a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial burial mounds. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered * Diameter: Approx. 66 meters * Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion * Width: Approx. 88 meters * Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Tenri City website [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]]

{{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 9.68 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 44.65 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial_structure = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South |label = Nishinorikura Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (前方後円墳) and part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}}. The site is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped burial mound constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}}, it forms a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} and {{ill|Shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, forming a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial burial mounds. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered * Diameter: Approx. 66 meters * Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion * Width: Approx. 88 meters * Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Tenri City website [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Keyhole-shaped burial mounds]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, forming a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial burial mounds. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered * Diameter: Approx. 66 meters * Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion * Width: Approx. 88 meters * Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - {{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 9.68 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 44.65 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial_structure = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South |label = Nishinorikura Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (前方後円墳) and part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}}. The site is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped burial mound constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}}, it forms a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} and {{ill|Shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, forming a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial burial mounds. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered * Diameter: Approx. 66 meters * Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion * Width: Approx. 88 meters * Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Tenri City website [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - revision.comment: Adding [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:横穴式石室]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 9.68 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 44.65 |ISO = JP-29 |shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial_structure = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South |label = Nishinorikura Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (前方後円墳) and part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}}. The site is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped burial mound constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}}, it forms a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} and {{ill|Shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, forming a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial burial mounds. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered * Diameter: Approx. 66 meters * Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion * Width: Approx. 88 meters * Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Tenri City website [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:横穴式石室]]

Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - {{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 9.68 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 44.65 |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳}} |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial_structure = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South |label = Nishinorikura Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (前方後円墳) and part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}}. The site is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped burial mound constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}}, it forms a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} and {{ill|Shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, forming a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial burial mounds. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered * Diameter: Approx. 66 meters * Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion * Width: Approx. 88 meters * Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 9.68 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 44.65 |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial_structure = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South |label = Nishinorikura Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (前方後円墳) and part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}}. The site is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped burial mound constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}}, it forms a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪}} and {{ill|Shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, forming a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial burial mounds. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered * Diameter: Approx. 66 meters * Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion * Width: Approx. 88 meters * Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Tenri City website [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 9.68 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 44.65 |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial_structure = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South |label = Nishinorikura Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} burial mound located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (前方後円墳) and part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}}. The site is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped burial mound constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}}, it forms a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|Shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, forming a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial burial mounds. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered * Diameter: Approx. 66 meters * Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion * Width: Approx. 88 meters * Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Tenri City website [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - revision.comment: Text replacement - "|古墳}}" to "|古墳|1=Kofun}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese Burial Mound |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 9.68 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 44.65 |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped burial mound|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial_structure = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |associated_mounds = |construction_period = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped burial mound - South |label = Nishinorikura Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} burial mound located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped burial mound (前方後円墳) and part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}}. The site is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped burial mound constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}}, it forms a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|Shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, forming a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial burial mounds. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered * Diameter: Approx. 66 meters * Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion * Width: Approx. 88 meters * Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Tenri City website [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:National Historic Sites in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Cultural Properties|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Cultural Properties)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Cultural Properties Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] -
