Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial_structure = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |label = Nishinorikura Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}}, it forms a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, forming a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == other_designation == === National Historic Site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City other_designation|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City other_designation)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National other_designation Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City other_designation|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City other_designation)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National other_designation Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 9.68 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 44.65 |ISO = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial_structure = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |label = Nishinorikura Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}}, it forms a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, forming a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == other_designation == === National Historic Site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City other_designation|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City other_designation)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National other_designation Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial_structure = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |label = Nishinorikura Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site is designated as a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}}, it forms a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, forming a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was designated a National {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == other_designation == === National Historic Site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic Site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of Historic Sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City other_designation|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City other_designation)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National other_designation Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 9.68 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 44.65 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial_structure = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |label = Nishinorikura Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site is designated as a National {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}}, it forms a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, forming a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was designated a National {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National other_designation on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of other_designations, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City other_designation|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City other_designation)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National other_designation Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 9.68 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 44.65 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial_structure = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |label = Nishinorikura Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site is designated as a National {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}}, it forms a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, forming a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. Artifacts such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was designated a National {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National other_designation on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of other_designations, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City other_designation|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City other_designation)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National other_designation Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National other_designation on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of other_designations, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City other_designation|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City other_designation)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National other_designation Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:New pages most likely untranslated or similar]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 35 |latitude_sec = 9.68 |longitude_deg = 135 |longitude_min = 50 |longitude_sec = 44.65 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial_structure = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |icon = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |label = Nishinorikura Kofun |label_position = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site is designation1 as a National {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}}, it forms a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, forming a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was designation1 a National {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National other_designation on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of other_designations, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City other_designation|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City other_designation)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National other_designation Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Tenri City website [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishinorikura Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishinorikura Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishinorikura Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]]

Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 9.68 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 44.65 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial_structure = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National {{ill|other_other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_other_designation|lt=other_other_designation of Japan}}} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |map_dot_label = Nishinorikura Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site is other_designation1 as a National {{ill|other_other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_other_designation|lt=other_other_designation of Japan}}} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}}, it types a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, typeing a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was other_designation1 a National {{ill|other_other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_other_designation|lt=other_other_designation of Japan}}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == other_other_designation == === National other_other_designation === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National other_other_designation on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded other_designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of other_other_designations, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City other_other_designation|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City other_other_designation)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National other_other_designation Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was other_designation1 a National {{ill|other_other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_other_designation|lt=other_other_designation of Japan}}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Designated a National other_other_designation on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded other_designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of other_other_designations, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City other_other_designation|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City other_other_designation)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National other_other_designation Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 9.68 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 44.65 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial_structure = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National {{ill|other_other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_other_designation|lt=other_other_designation of Japan}}} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |map_dot_label = Nishinorikura Kofun |map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site is other_designation1 as a National {{ill|other_other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_other_designation|lt=other_other_designation of Japan}}} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}}, it types a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, typeing a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was other_designation1 a National {{ill|other_other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_other_designation|lt=other_other_designation of Japan}}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

Designated a National other_other_designation on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded other_designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of other_other_designations, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City other_other_designation|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City other_other_designation)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National other_other_designation Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 9.68 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 44.65 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped Kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial_structure = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |map_dot_label = Nishinorikura Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri City|ja|天理市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site is other_designation1 as a National {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}}, it types a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, typeing a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}. burial goods such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was other_designation1 a National {{ill|other_designation|ja|史跡|1=other_designation|lt=other_designation of Japan}}} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound Structure == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == other_designation == === National other_designation === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National other_designation on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded other_designation of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the Designation of other_designations, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City other_designation|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City other_designation)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Group|ja|杣之内古墳群|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}} ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳}} * {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National other_designation Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Tenri City website [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Tenri City]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:National other_designations in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishinorikura Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishinorikura Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nishinorikura Kofun}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:qq]]
