Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |map_dot_label = Nishinorikura Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site is Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}}, it types a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, typeing a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was Historic site a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound burial == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length Ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, typeing a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was Historic site a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound burial == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length Ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 9.68 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 44.65 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |map_dot_label = Nishinorikura Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site is Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}}, it types a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, typeing a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was Historic site a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound burial == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length Ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. {{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 9.68 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 44.65 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |map_dot_label = Nishinorikura Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site is Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}}, it types a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, typeing a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was Historic site a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound burial == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length Ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 9.68 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 44.65 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |map_dot_label = Nishinorikura Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site is Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}}, it types a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, typeing a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was Historic site a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound burial == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length Ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Tenri City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]]

Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - 墳丘
（右に前方部、左奥に後円部、手前に平坦地） |別名 = |所属 = [[杣之内古墳群]] |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町字乗鞍 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 9.68 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 50|経度秒 = 44.65 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長118m
高さ16m（前方部） |埋葬施設 = （推定）[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = [[埴輪]]・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「西乗鞍古墳」
（「杣之内古墳群」に包含） |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南 |ラベル = 西乗鞍古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''西乗鞍古墳'''（にしのりくらこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[杣之内古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「杣之内古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]東縁、東から西に延びる丘陵の麓を切断して築造された大型前方後円墳である。[[東乗鞍古墳]]・[[小墓古墳]]などとともに杣之内古墳群のうちの一群を形成する。これまでに4次の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南方向に向ける。墳丘は2段築成の可能性が高いとされる。墳丘外表では[[円筒埴輪]]・[[形象埴輪]]が検出されている。また墳丘くびれ部東側・西側には[[造出]]を有する{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。墳丘周囲には周濠（内濠）・外堤が巡らされており（現在では埋没して平坦地を形成）、これらを含めた全長は南北約165メートルにもおよぶ。さらに南側・東側では外濠（掘割）も認められ、二重周濠とも見られる{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}。埋葬施設は明らかでないが、[[横穴式石室]]の可能性が指摘される。副葬品も詳らかでないが、埴輪以外の出土品として[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]が検出されている。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期末葉の[[5世紀]]末葉頃と推定される史跡説明板。。同時期の古墳としては大王墓に次ぐ規模の古墳になるが、付近では後期前半の古墳として東乗鞍古墳・小墓古墳も知られ、これらを[[物部氏]]の首長墓とする説が挙げられている[[白石太一郎]] 「古墳からみた物部氏」『古墳の被葬者を推理する（中公叢書）』 中央公論新社、2018年、pp. 253-260。。 古墳域は、[[2018年]]（[[平成]]30年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1893年]]（[[明治]]26年）の『大和國古墳墓取調書』に絵図{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 * [[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）、[[陸軍特別大演習]]の際に[[昭和天皇]]が墳丘上（乗鞍山野外統監部{{Cite book |和書 |author=原武史 |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=72 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4}}）で全軍を統監。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年）、墳丘測量調査（[[天理大学]]歴史研究会）{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳発掘調査現地説明会資料|2014}}。 * [[1981年]]（昭和56年）、南側隣接地の発掘調査：第1次調査（[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]）{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳発掘調査現地説明会資料|2014}}。 * [[1999年]]（[[平成]]11年）、墳丘西側平坦地の発掘調査：第2次調査（天理市教育委員会、[[2000年]]に概報刊行・[[2016年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳発掘調査現地説明会資料|2014}}。 * [[2012年]]度（平成24年度）、発掘調査：第3次調査（天理市教育委員会、2016年に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳発掘調査現地説明会資料|2014}}。 * [[2014年]]度（平成26年度）、範囲確認発掘調査：第4次調査（天理市教育委員会、2016年に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳発掘調査現地説明会資料|2014}}。 * [[2015年]]度（平成27年度）、航空レーザー測量調査（天理市教育委員会、2016年に報告書刊行）。 * [[2018年]]（平成30年）2月13日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「[[杣之内古墳群]]」のうち）。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}。 * 墳丘長：約118メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成か。 ** 直径：約66メートル ** 高さ：約15.5メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：約88メートル ** 高さ：約16メートル 墳丘長は、杣之内古墳群では[[西山古墳]]に次ぐ2番目の規模になる。墳丘裾部西側には東西約20-30メートル・南北約130メートルの基壇状平坦地が認められる{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}。発掘調査によればこの平坦地は周濠（内濠）・外堤が埋没して形成されたものとされる{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}。また平坦地のさらに南側・東側では掘割が検出されており、これを外濠とする二重周濠の可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}。 Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|前方部墳頂の大元帥陛下駐蹕之處碑 西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|円筒埴輪
{{small|唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}} == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 西乗鞍古墳（史跡「[[杣之内古墳群]]」のうち） - 2018年（平成30年）2月13日に既指定の史跡「[[西山古墳]]」に西乗鞍古墳を追加指定して「杣之内古墳群」に名称変更[https://web.archive.org/web/20171117134715/http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html 史跡等の指定等について]（文化庁報道発表、2017年11月17日）。平成30年2月13日文部科学省告示第19号。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（天理市教育委員会、2019年設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** 概説書 *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=西乗鞍古墳|title=天理市の文化財|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|pages=|ref={{Harvid|西乗鞍古墳（天理市の文化財）|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=西乗鞍古墳|title=天理の古墳100|publisher=天理市教育委員会|pages=|ref={{Harvid|西乗鞍古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}}} ** 発掘調査関係 *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/269 天理市埋蔵文化財調査概報（平成11年度・国庫補助事業） -柳本藩邸遺跡（第6次） 西乗鞍古墳-]|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref={{Harvid|天理市埋蔵文化財調査概報（平成11年度・国庫補助事業）|2000}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 *** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/47/kohun_nishinorikura_siryou20141108.pdf 「西乗鞍古墳発掘調査現地説明会資料」]}}（天理市教育委員会文化財課、2014年）。|ref={{Harvid|西乗鞍古墳発掘調査現地説明会資料|2014}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=木下亘|year=1989|chapter=西乗鞍古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|西乗鞍古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=山内紀嗣|year=2014|chapter=西乗鞍古墳と東乗鞍古墳|title=[https://opac.tenri-u.ac.jp/opac/repository/metadata/3280/?opkey=R157834405201772&idx=4 杣之内古墳群の研究]|publisher=杣之内古墳群研究会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}}} - リンクは天理大学学術情報リポジトリ。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]|author=|year=1982|chapter=|title=天理市西乗鞍古墳南遺跡発掘調査報告 -天理市立山の辺運動場建設に伴う発掘調査-|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|title=奈良県天理市峯塚古墳・西乗鞍古墳・鑵子塚古墳測量調査報告（天理大学歴史研究会調査研究報告 1）|publisher=天理大学歴史研究会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=杣之内古墳群I 西乗鞍古墳（天理市埋蔵文化財調査報告 第10集）|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=|title=西乗鞍古墳後円部墳頂および東斜面における物理探査|date=2017|publisher=[[天理大学]]文学部歴史文化学科考古学・民俗学専攻|journal=古事 -天理大学考古学・民俗学研究室紀要-|volume=|number=21|pages=42-50|url=https://opac.tenri-u.ac.jp/opac/repository/metadata/4377|doi=|ref=}} - リンクは天理大学学術情報リポジトリ。 * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=西乗鞍古墳|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群]|publisher=天理市教育委員会|pages=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 関連項目 == * [[杣之内古墳群]] ** [[東乗鞍古墳]] ** [[小墓古墳]] * [[夜都岐神社]] * [[物部氏]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1958|杣之内古墳群}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html 西乗鞍古墳] - （「杣之内古墳群」に包含） |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |アイコン = 前方後円墳-南 |ラベル = 西乗鞍古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''西乗鞍古墳'''（にしのりくらこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[天理市]]杣之内町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。[[杣之内古墳群]]を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（史跡「杣之内古墳群」のうち）。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]東縁、東から西に延びる丘陵の麓を切断して築造された大型前方後円墳である。[[東乗鞍古墳]]・[[小墓古墳]]などとともに杣之内古墳群のうちの一群を形成する。これまでに4次の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を南方向に向ける。墳丘は2段築成の可能性が高いとされる。墳丘外表では[[円筒埴輪]]・[[形象埴輪]]が検出されている。また墳丘くびれ部東側・西側には[[造出]]を有する{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。墳丘周囲には周濠（内濠）・外堤が巡らされており（現在では埋没して平坦地を形成）、これらを含めた全長は南北約165メートルにもおよぶ。さらに南側・東側では外濠（掘割）も認められ、二重周濠とも見られる{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}。埋葬施設は明らかでないが、[[横穴式石室]]の可能性が指摘される。副葬品も詳らかでないが、埴輪以外の出土品として[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]]が検出されている。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期末葉の[[5世紀]]末葉頃と推定される史跡説明板。。同時期の古墳としては大王墓に次ぐ規模の古墳になるが、付近では後期前半の古墳として東乗鞍古墳・小墓古墳も知られ、これらを[[物部氏]]の首長墓とする説が挙げられている[[白石太一郎]] 「古墳からみた物部氏」『古墳の被葬者を推理する（中公叢書）』 中央公論新社、2018年、pp. 253-260。。 古墳域は、[[2018年]]（[[平成]]30年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1893年]]（[[明治]]26年）の『大和國古墳墓取調書』に絵図{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}。 * [[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）、[[陸軍特別大演習]]の際に[[昭和天皇]]が墳丘上（乗鞍山野外統監部{{Cite book |和書 |author=原武史 |title=昭和天皇御召列車全記録 |publisher=新潮社 |year=2016-09-30 |page=72 |isbn=978-4-10-320523-4}}）で全軍を統監。 * [[1970年]]（昭和45年）、墳丘測量調査（[[天理大学]]歴史研究会）{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳発掘調査現地説明会資料|2014}}。 * [[1981年]]（昭和56年）、南側隣接地の発掘調査：第1次調査（[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]）{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳発掘調査現地説明会資料|2014}}。 * [[1999年]]（[[平成]]11年）、墳丘西側平坦地の発掘調査：第2次調査（天理市教育委員会、[[2000年]]に概報刊行・[[2016年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳発掘調査現地説明会資料|2014}}。 * [[2012年]]度（平成24年度）、発掘調査：第3次調査（天理市教育委員会、2016年に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳発掘調査現地説明会資料|2014}}。 * [[2014年]]度（平成26年度）、範囲確認発掘調査：第4次調査（天理市教育委員会、2016年に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳発掘調査現地説明会資料|2014}}。 * [[2015年]]度（平成27年度）、航空レーザー測量調査（天理市教育委員会、2016年に報告書刊行）。 * [[2018年]]（平成30年）2月13日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（史跡「[[杣之内古墳群]]」のうち）。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}。 * 墳丘長：約118メートル * 後円部 - 2段築成か。 ** 直径：約66メートル ** 高さ：約15.5メートル * 前方部 ** 幅：約88メートル ** 高さ：約16メートル 墳丘長は、杣之内古墳群では[[西山古墳]]に次ぐ2番目の規模になる。墳丘裾部西側には東西約20-30メートル・南北約130メートルの基壇状平坦地が認められる{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}。発掘調査によればこの平坦地は周濠（内濠）・外堤が埋没して形成されたものとされる{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}。また平坦地のさらに南側・東側では掘割が検出されており、これを外濠とする二重周濠の可能性が指摘される{{Sfn|西乗鞍古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}。 Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|前方部墳頂の大元帥陛下駐蹕之處碑 西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|円筒埴輪
{{small|唐古・鍵考古学ミュージアム企画展示時に撮影。}} == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 西乗鞍古墳（史跡「[[杣之内古墳群]]」のうち） - 2018年（平成30年）2月13日に既指定の史跡「[[西山古墳]]」に西乗鞍古墳を追加指定して「杣之内古墳群」に名称変更[https://web.archive.org/web/20171117134715/http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html 史跡等の指定等について]（文化庁報道発表、2017年11月17日）。平成30年2月13日文部科学省告示第19号。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（天理市教育委員会、2019年設置） * 地方自治体発行 ** 概説書 *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=西乗鞍古墳|title=天理市の文化財|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|pages=|ref={{Harvid|西乗鞍古墳（天理市の文化財）|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=西乗鞍古墳|title=天理の古墳100|publisher=天理市教育委員会|pages=|ref={{Harvid|西乗鞍古墳（天理の古墳100）|2015}}}} ** 発掘調査関係 *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/269 天理市埋蔵文化財調査概報（平成11年度・国庫補助事業） -柳本藩邸遺跡（第6次） 西乗鞍古墳-]|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|page=|ref={{Harvid|天理市埋蔵文化財調査概報（平成11年度・国庫補助事業）|2000}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 *** {{Wikicite|reference={{PDFlink|[http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/ikkrwebBrowse/material/files/group/47/kohun_nishinorikura_siryou20141108.pdf 「西乗鞍古墳発掘調査現地説明会資料」]}}（天理市教育委員会文化財課、2014年）。|ref={{Harvid|西乗鞍古墳発掘調査現地説明会資料|2014}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=木下亘|year=1989|chapter=西乗鞍古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|西乗鞍古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=山内紀嗣|year=2014|chapter=西乗鞍古墳と東乗鞍古墳|title=[https://opac.tenri-u.ac.jp/opac/repository/metadata/3280/?opkey=R157834405201772&idx=4 杣之内古墳群の研究]|publisher=杣之内古墳群研究会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|杣之内古墳群の研究|2014}}}} - リンクは天理大学学術情報リポジトリ。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]|author=|year=1982|chapter=|title=天理市西乗鞍古墳南遺跡発掘調査報告 -天理市立山の辺運動場建設に伴う発掘調査-|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|title=奈良県天理市峯塚古墳・西乗鞍古墳・鑵子塚古墳測量調査報告（天理大学歴史研究会調査研究報告 1）|publisher=天理大学歴史研究会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2016|chapter=|title=杣之内古墳群I 西乗鞍古墳（天理市埋蔵文化財調査報告 第10集）|publisher=天理市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=|title=西乗鞍古墳後円部墳頂および東斜面における物理探査|date=2017|publisher=[[天理大学]]文学部歴史文化学科考古学・民俗学専攻|journal=古事 -天理大学考古学・民俗学研究室紀要-|volume=|number=21|pages=42-50|url=https://opac.tenri-u.ac.jp/opac/repository/metadata/4377|doi=|ref=}} - リンクは天理大学学術情報リポジトリ。 * {{Cite book|和書|year=2021|chapter=西乗鞍古墳|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/94674 物部氏の古墳 杣之内古墳群]|publisher=天理市教育委員会|pages=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 関連項目 == * [[杣之内古墳群]] ** [[東乗鞍古墳]] ** [[小墓古墳]] * [[夜都岐神社]] * [[物部氏]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|1958|杣之内古墳群}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html 西乗鞍古墳] - {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 9.68 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 44.65 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |map_dot_label = Nishinorikura Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site is Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}}, it types a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, typeing a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was Historic site a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound burial == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length Ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Tenri City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:西乗鞍古墳]] {{translated page|ja|西乗鞍古墳|version=104551277|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 9.68 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 44.65 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |map_dot_label = Nishinorikura Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site is Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}}, it types a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, typeing a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was Historic site a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound burial == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length Ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Tenri City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:西乗鞍古墳]] {{translated page|ja|西乗鞍古墳|version=104551277|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:天理市の歴史]]
