revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[ja:西乗鞍古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.586022222222|N|135.84573611111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11523653}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q81256722]] Wikidata name: Nishinorikura Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 9.68 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 44.65 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |map_dot_label = Nishinorikura Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site is Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}}, it types a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, typeing a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was Historic site a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound burial == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length Ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 9.68 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 44.65 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |map_dot_label = Nishinorikura Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site is Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}}, it types a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, typeing a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was Historic site a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound burial == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length Ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. {{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 9.68 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 44.65 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |map_dot_label = Nishinorikura Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site is Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}}, it types a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, typeing a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was Historic site a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound burial == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length Ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa

{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Tenri City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:西乗鞍古墳]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|西乗鞍古墳|version=104551277|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] {{wikidata link|Q81256722}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[ja:西乗鞍古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.586022222222|N|135.84573611111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11523653}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q81256722]] Wikidata name: Nishinorikura Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 9.68 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 44.65 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |map_dot_label = Nishinorikura Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site is Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}}, it types a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, typeing a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was Historic site a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound burial == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length Ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - {{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - [[commons:Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[ja:西乗鞍古墳]] [[commons:Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[ja:西乗鞍古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[ja:西乗鞍古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.586022222222|N|135.84573611111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11523653}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q81256722]] Wikidata name: Nishinorikura Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 9.68 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 44.65 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |map_dot_label = Nishinorikura Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site is Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}}, it types a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, typeing a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was Historic site a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound burial == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length Ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Tenri City website {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:西乗鞍古墳]] {{translated page|ja|西乗鞍古墳|version=104551277|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q81256722}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[ja:西乗鞍古墳]] [[commons:Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[ja:西乗鞍古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[ja:西乗鞍古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.586022222222|N|135.84573611111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11523653}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q81256722]] Wikidata name: Nishinorikura Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 9.68 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 44.65 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |map_dot_label = Nishinorikura Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site is Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}}, it types a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, typeing a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was Historic site a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound burial == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length Ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[ja:西乗鞍古墳]] [[commons:Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[ja:西乗鞍古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nishinorikura Kofun]][[ja:西乗鞍古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|WD=Q502196}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.586022222222|N|135.84573611111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11523653}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q81256722]] Wikidata name: Nishinorikura Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Nishinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Nishinorikura Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Front rectangular portion on the right, rear circular portion in the left background, and flatland in the foreground) |alt_name = |group = [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] |location = [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], Somanouchi Town, Norikura District |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 9.68 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 44.65 |omitted = JP-29 |type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] |size = Mound length: 118m
Height: 16m (front rectangular portion) |burial = Presumed {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]], [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |associated_mounds = |built = Late 5th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} (part of "Somanouchi Kofun Group") |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - South |map_dot_label = Nishinorikura Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Nishinorikura Kofun''' (西乗鞍古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] Kofun located in [[:en:Tenri, Nara|Tenri City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and part of the [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]]. The site is Historic site as a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} within the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Overview == Nishinorikura Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped Kofun constructed at the base of a ridge extending east to west along the eastern edge of the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. Together with the {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}}, it types a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. To date, four excavation surveys have been conducted. The mound is oriented southward, with the front rectangular portion facing that direction. It is believed to have a two-tier construction. The mound's surface has revealed {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} and {{ill|shaped haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaped haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}. The mound also has projections on the eastern and western sides of the constriction area. Surrounding the mound are a moat (inner moat) and an embankment, typeing a flat area (now buried) that extends approximately 165 meters in total length from north to south. An outer moat has also been identified on the southern and eastern sides, suggesting a double moat system. The burial chamber is presumed to be a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}. burial goods such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] have also been discovered. The construction period is estimated to be the late 5th century, near the end of the {{ill|Middle Kofun period|ja|古墳時代中期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Middle Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Explanation sign on-site. This mound is one of the largest from its time, second only to imperial Kofuns. Nearby, Higashinorikura Kofun and Obaka Kofun date to the early Late Kofun period, and these mounds are hypothesized to be burial sites for the leaders of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} In 2018, Nishinorikura Kofun was Historic site a National [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} as part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group." == Mound burial == The mound's area are as follows:{{Sfn|Somanouchi Kofun Group Study|2014}}{{Sfn|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} * Mound length: Approx. 118 meters * Rear circular portion: Possibly two-tiered ** Diameter: Approx. 66 meters ** Height: Approx. 15.5 meters * Front rectangular portion ** Width: Approx. 88 meters ** Height: Approx. 16 meters The mound length Ranks second within the Somanouchi Kofun Group, following {{ill|Nishiyama Kofun|ja|西山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishiyama Kofun|qq=}}. A flat area resembling a base extends approximately 20–30 meters east-west and 130 meters north-south along the western edge of the mound. Excavation suggests that this flat area resulted from the burial of the inner moat and embankment. An outer moat has also been discovered, indicating the potential presence of a double moat system. File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - File:Nishinorikura Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front rectangular portion toward the rear circular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular portion toward the front rectangular portion File:Nishinorikura Kofun, sekihi.jpg|Monument on the front rectangular portion's summit File:西乗鞍古墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|Cylindrical haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the Karako Kagi Archaeological Museum.}} == Historic site == === National Historic site === * Nishinorikura Kofun (part of the "Somanouchi Kofun Group") - Designated a National Historic site on February 13, 2018, as part of the expanded Historic site of the preexisting "Nishiyama Kofun" to include Nishinorikura Kofun and rename it to "Somanouchi Kofun Group."[http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/1397922.html Regarding the designation1 of Historic sites, etc.] (Cultural Affairs Agency, Press Release, November 17, 2017).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science, and Technology Notification No. 19, February 13, 2018. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * Explanation sign on-site (installed by Tenri City Board of Education, 2019). * Local Government Publications ** Overview Books *** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic site)|1990}}}} *** {{Cite book|和書|year=2015|chapter=Nishinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri Kofun 100|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Nishinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Somanouchi Kofun cluster|Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] ** {{ill|Higashinorikura Kofun|ja|東乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinorikura Kofun|qq=}} ** {{ill|Obaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obaka Kofun|qq=}} * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nishinorikura Kofun}} * {{National Historic site Database|401|1958|Somanouchi Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/miryoku/tenrinomiryoku1/kofun/1552537126019.html Nishinorikura Kofun] - Tenri City website {{Kofun navbar}} [[ja:西乗鞍古墳]] {{translated page|ja|西乗鞍古墳|version=104551277|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q81256722}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
